description: Changing assertions to match broken output masks regressions.
body:
Only update expectations after confirming the contract changed intentionally.

Use only as a diagnostic do-not-use signal; do not expose this as positive strategy, methodology, or memory. Use when a proposed approach skips test runner output, fixture intent, coverage boundaries, flake history, and negative paths or claims success without domain evidence. Do not use this dossier as a positive implementation strategy; it is a do-not-use signal. Actions: block the shortcut, name the missing evidence, and switch to a safer methodology or ask for human approval. Validation signals include absence or contradiction in focused test output, regression tests, fixture assertions, and rerun evidence. Risk boundary: treat repeated recurrence as a blocker when it can cause testing implementation details only, masking flakes, and claiming coverage without behavior proof.
